text: Uptime SLA: 99.9% monthly, measured as (total minutes − downtime)/total minutes, excluding scheduled maintenance with advance notice. Definitions for outage vs. degradation are documented. Historical uptime and incidents are published on a public status page.
Performance SLAs/SLOs include p95 latency targets, throughput limits, and error rate thresholds with dashboards for real-time monitoring. Degraded performance is explicitly defined and tracked.

Support tiers: Critical (≤1 hour response, 24x7), High (≤4 hours), Medium (≤1 business day), Low (≤2 business days). Clear escalation paths and on-call procedures are documented, including incident communication cadence.

SLA breaches trigger service credits based on severity/duration (e.g., tiered % credits). Credits are automatically applied or available upon request. Repeated breaches grant termination rights. Post-incident RCAs and corrective actions are provided.
We offer a 99.9% uptime SLA, but definitions for downtime, degradation, and measurement methodology are not fully specified.
We monitor performance, but do not publish concrete latency/error SLOs or dashboards for customers.
Support response times are defined, but escalation paths, on-call coverage, or resolution targets are not clearly documented.
Service credits may be available for outages, but calculation methods and application (automatic vs. request) are unclear.
We provide best-effort availability without formal SLAs or guarantees.
We offer SLAs but do not provide service credits or remedies if they are missed.
We guarantee 99.9% uptime without defining how it is measured or calculated.
